segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Fishermen and sailors sometimes claim to have seen monsters in the sea.

translations.zh-Hans: 渔民和水手有时声称自己在海里见过怪物。

segments.text: Though people have often laughed at stories told by seamen, it is now known that many of these which have at times been sighted are simply strange fish.

translations.zh-Hans: 虽然人们常嘲笑水手讲的故事，但现在大家知道，那些不时被人看到的“怪物”中，有许多其实只是奇特的鱼类。

segments.text: Occasionally, unusual creatures are washed to the shore, but they are rarely caught out at sea.

translations.zh-Hans: 偶尔会有罕见生物被海浪冲上岸，却很少在外海被捕获。

segments.text: Some time ago, however, a peculiar fish was caught near Madagascar.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而不久前，人们在马达加斯加附近捕到了一条奇怪的鱼。

segments.text: A small fishing boat was carried miles out to sea by the powerful fish as it pulled on the line.

translations.zh-Hans: 这条力大无穷的鱼咬住鱼线后，把一条小渔船拖到了几英里外的海面。

segments.text: Realizing that this was no ordinary fish, the fishermen made every effort not to damage it in any way.

translations.zh-Hans: 渔民们意识到这绝非普通的鱼，于是千方百计不让它受到任何伤害。

segments.text: When it was eventually brought to shore, it was found to be over 13 feet long.

translations.zh-Hans: 当它最终被带上岸时，人们发现它身长超过13英尺。

segments.text: It had a head like a horse, big blue eyes, shining silver skin, and a bright red tail.

translations.zh-Hans: 它长着马一样的头、大大的蓝眼睛、闪亮的银色皮肤和鲜红的尾巴。

segments.text: The fish, which has since been sent to a museum where it is being examined by a scientist, is called an oarfish.

translations.zh-Hans: 这条鱼叫皇带鱼，后来被送往博物馆，由一名科学家进行研究。

segments.text: Such creatures have rarely been seen alive by man as they live at a depth of 600 feet.

translations.zh-Hans: 人们极少见到这种生物活着的样子，因为它们生活在600英尺深的水下。
